{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 0.95,
    "R1.7": 0.6,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.8,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The output asserts novelty by claiming CalibSynth is the first principled treatment of synthesis-time uncertainty in program induction, but this claim is disputable given existing literature on calibration of code/program models and selective prediction.",
    "R1.7": "The output flags one missing citation for temperature scaling work by Guo et al. (2017), but does not audit every load-bearing citation.",
    "RX.1": "The output does not mark confidence or missing evidence for each recommendation, only providing a general score for the system's contribution.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free but with one minor regression: it introduces new errors by overclaiming novelty and conflating contributions.",
    "RX.5": "The output blends sources without traceability; major rewrites are not annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.8,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 1,
    "RX.4": 0.8,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "blockers": []
}
